Initially, they maintain morale by rehashing old stories and reciting inside jokes, but the gravity of their situation sinks in as a return to civilization becomes more unlikely. As the sheer scope, hypnotic pull and spirit-crushing mirages of the desert affect the men, their will to live and sense of identity is challenged and transformed.

programme.title: A Slice of Romance
programme.desc: PR exec Mikayla strikes a deal with pastry chef Austin to rebrand his café to get dating advice.

programme.title: A Royal Makeover
programme.desc: When Ramone discovers he is about to be King, he takes lessons from royal expert Angelica.

programme.title: Falcon Rising
programme.desc: Former Marine John "Falcon" Chapman is a dark anti-hero driven by guilt and suffering from PTSD, who will destroy himself unless given something else to destroy - a useful weapon-of-last-resort for the U.S. State Department.

programme.title: Just Getting Started
programme.desc: Duke Diver is living the high life as the freewheeling manager of a luxurious resort in Palm Springs, Calif. He soon faces competition from Leo, a former military man who likes the same woman that Duke is interested in. When Diver's past suddenly catches up with him, he must put aside his differences and reluctantly team up with Leo to stop whoever is trying to kill him.

programme.title: Hard Lesson
programme.desc: A Jewish parole officer, Jeff Solomon White, struggles in his new role as a reading teacher for illiterate teens at a New York high school. Raised by African American parents, White sees the situation differently and is motivated to fight for his students, who face an almost certain life of crime and prison.

programme.title: Take Back
programme.desc: Zara (Gillian White) and Brian (Michael Jai White) are living the perfect small town life when a mysterious stranger from Zara's secret past kidnaps their daughter and now they must race to save her life.

programme.title: Rage: Kill or Be Killed
programme.desc: Jackie “Labrae” Robinson dominates Houston’s unsanctioned boxing circuit—until he comes face-to-face with the one opponent, he never imagined fighting: his personal hero, legendary champion Theo Barnes. However, Theo is a haunted man dealing with his own demons and an advanced case of CTE. A first meeting quickly escalates into an all-out brawl, making the stakes of two champion fighters that much higher.

programme.title: Shotlanta
programme.desc: Mozzy, driven by a desire to lift his family out of poverty in Jamaica, emigrates to Atlanta. He quickly rises in the ranks of the city's drug trade, determined to achieve wealth and security for his mother and sister. However, his ruthless pursuit of success puts him on a dangerous path, where the line between ambition and destruction blurs.

programme.title: Misfortune
programme.desc: When tragedy hits, Rachel is left with more than regret, leaving her bound to a wheelchair and her best friend Sammy's brother Brian with a brain injury. Looking for a new outlook on life, Rachel finds comfort in a new home with more than she bargained for in the form of a fortune teller arcade game, offering her one wish. Will she succeed in bringing her friends back from a detrimental past or will it be misfortune for them all?

programme.title: Bruised
programme.desc: Young and beautiful Kayla Brown discovers that her perfect husband is not who she thought he was. Fearing for her life, she must do the unthinkable to end her nightmarish marriage, SHE MUST KILL HIM.

programme.title: Wish
programme.desc: Wish is a beautiful, confident, woman, who makes no apologies for who she is and what she does. Very sexually free, Wish goes after what she wants. If she feels like making out with a total stranger, she will. If she feels like bringing home the waitress, she will, and she will do it with the ruthlessness of a man, but with the finesse of a woman. Wish is a force to be reckoned with, until one day she meets a woman who turns her world upside down, and nothing will ever be the same.

programme.title: The Wait Game
programme.desc: Yolanda Hills, a woman deeply in love with her longtime partner Jonas Jones, has been patiently awaiting their wedding day. Their idyllic relationship takes a terrifying turn when a home intruder invades their peaceful sanctuary. Held captive in her own home, Yolanda is confronted with a shocking revelation that will forever alter the course of their lives.

programme.title: 500 MPH Storm
programme.desc: A scientist must get his family to safety before a "hypercane" wipes America off the map.

programme.title: Ape x Mecha Ape: New World Order
programme.desc: Mankind's last hope rests on Abraham and Mecha Ape MkII to defeat a new enemy.

programme.title: 100 Ghost Street: The Return of Richard Speck
programme.desc: Paranormal investigators confront the ghost of Richard Speck at the site of his heinous crimes.

programme.title: Hillside Cannibals
programme.desc: A true story that inspired THE HILLS HAVE EYES of a family who lived in caves and ate the flesh of passersby.

programme.title: Devil's Triangle
programme.desc: Marine biologists crash in the Bermuda Triangle, discover they have stumbled into the lost city of Atlantis.

programme.title: Camp Cold Brook (Edited Version)
programme.desc: TV producer Jack Wilson (Chad Michael Murray) fears his paranormal reality show will be cancelled. Desperate to increase the ratings, Wilson takes his filming crew out to investigate the legendary Camp Cold Brook--an abandoned summer camp where a mass murder had taken place decades ago. They soon realize they are not alone

programme.title: The Bride with White Hair II
programme.desc: On Kit and Lyre's wedding night, Lien Ni-Chang, the bride with white hair kidnaps Lyre and takes her to her harem of fighting women to indoctrinate her against Kit. Kit tries to lead a rescue party and go up against the formidable enemies.

programme.title: BODY BAGS (Edited Version)
programme.desc: Body Bags is an anthology horror film made up of three stories bookended by a creepy morgue assistant played by John Carpenter himself. The first story, The Gas Station, features Robert Carradine as a serial killer, with cameos by Sam Raimi and Wes Craven. Hair follows Stacy Keach as he receives a botched hair transplant that infests him with an alien parasite, and Eye is another transplant story, this time featuring Mark Hamill as a baseball player who loses an eye in a car accident and receives a transplant, only to be overtaken with the personality of the eye's previous owner: a murderous misogynist.

programme.title: Ice Cream Man (Edited Version)
programme.desc: Poor Gregory. After being released from the Wishing Well Sanatorium, all he wants to do is make the children happy. So Gregory reopens the old ice cream factory, and all the unappreciative brats are reprocessed into the flavor of the day.

programme.title: Little Dead Rotting Hood (Edited Version)
programme.desc: For years, the old woman in the woods has secretly kept the wolves of the forest at bay. But when the woman dies, the creatures suddenly attack the residents of a nearby small town. As more and more people turn up dead, the townsfolk discover something far more sinister than wolves lurking in the backwoods, and fight to save their home from evil by waging a bloody battle against the ruthless creatures. But as the wolves begin to overpower the town, only the womans granddaughter may have the key to stopping the bloodbath before its too late.

programme.title: A Creepshow Holiday Special
programme.desc: Robert Weston has reason to believe he might be a werewolf, but when he goes to a Shapeshifters Anonymous support group, he learns there's something far more sinister than his curse to worry about.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: After the death of a loved one, a drinking habit becomes insatiable. Then, dolls are Evie's favorite companions, but when a severed toy head appears in her dollhouse, Evie can no longer protect them.

programme.title: Creepshow
programme.desc: A platoon of American soldiers finds something more sinister than war behind enemy lines. Then, a man with a passion for found objects finds something particularly odd: a finger ... and it's growing.

programme.title: Tales from the Crypt
programme.desc: Electrician Niles Talbot has been promoted to executioner. When the death penalty is abolished in his state, Niles loses his job. But he decides to become a vigilante, punishing criminals that are released by justice system.

programme.title: Tales from the Crypt
programme.desc: Elizabeth kills her second husband for his insurance money. Upon getting rid of the body, she is attacked by an escaped mental patient dressed as Santa Claus. She soon fears that her young daughter may be Santa's next victim.

programme.title: Tales from the Crypt
programme.desc: A carnival daredevil is buried alive for his grand finale. He tells the viewers how he had undergone a doctor's experiment to transfer a cat's nine lives to him. He suddenly remembers that he may have miscounted just how many lives he has left.

programme.title: Tales from the Crypt
programme.desc: Sylvia Vane is a young, narcissistic prostitute, "sells" her beauty to a voodoo-dabbling pawnbroker so that she can use the money to snag a rich bachelor, Ronnie Price. She soon regrets it when her face begins to unexpectedly age rapidly.

programme.title: Tales from the Crypt
programme.desc: Peggy and Charles, newlyweds end up spending the night inside a decrepit mansion after a car accident. Charles plans to kill Peggy for her inheritance, but he soon learns that Peggy's family has a dark past. Peggy is not who she seems to be.

programme.title: Tales from the Crypt
programme.desc: An uptight elderly man named Jonas is forced into retirement, and soon discovers his wife Anita's obsession with adopting animals into their home, leading him to use the animals for a hobby of his own.

programme.title: Tales from the Crypt
programme.desc: In 1950, gold-digging secretary Cathy Finch marries Charlie Marno, an overweight, unhygienic, and grotesque slob of a man after being told by a fortune teller, Madame Vorna, that he will die shortly after inheriting a large sum of money.

programme.title: Tales from the Crypt
programme.desc: A dying elderly bachelor proposes to his crush, but is rejected for his age. He ends up meeting a discredited doctor who helps him switch bodies. In the end he discovers that his crush's affection comes at a price he cannot afford.

programme.title: Tales from the Crypt
programme.desc: Reno Crevice and Sam Forney, a pair of rival gamblers who hate each other with a passion, face off in a series of increasingly dangerous and gruesome games in order to see who will leave town when all is said and done.

programme.title: Tales from the Crypt
programme.desc: Land owner woos snobby, and wealthy woman to help finance a development project. He wins her over with a potion from his ex, voodoo priestess, that contains a deadly warning - resulting in an undead Margaret amorously chasing him without end.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Pygmalion and Elly" - Sonny Drysdale decides he needs to be Pygmalion to Elly's Galatea and remake her from a hillbilly into a woman of society, while Granny uses love charms to heat up their relationship.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Elly's First Date" - Mr. Drysdale convinces his self-absorbed stepson Sonny to date Elly May to Mrs. Drysdale's consternation, while Granny and Jed make preparations for Thanksgiving.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Jethro Goes to School" - Jed enrolls Jethro at an exclusive Beverly Hills elementary school. Millicent Schuyler-Potts, the proprietor of the school, is aghast when Jed and Jethro show up, convinced this is part of some ghastly hoax. After Jed says that banker Milburn Drysdale is his neighbor, she calls the banker. It turns out that Drysdale's bank holds the mortgage on the school. Schulyer-Potts suddenly warms to the charms of the Clampetts, even if it means accepting Jethro as a fifth grader.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Servants" - Ellie May starts wearing dresses but takes it as an insult when someone says she looks like a city girl. Mr. Drysdale talks Jed into taking in his butler and upstairs girl in a plan to civilize the Clampetts, but they don't quite understand the idea of servants. Miss Hathaway takes Jethro bowling and begins to show an interest in him.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Trick or Treat" - Granny wants to go home because "folks is so unfriendly, no one has come to call." Jed convinces her that they should call on them, and they find that people welcome them and give them all manner of gifts when they show up on Halloween in their normal hillbilly clothes. Also, Jed works on getting Pearl to come visit while she's trying to get hitched to the oilman and find a young man for Jethrine.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Race for Queen" - Elly May competes in a contest to determine "The Queen of Beverly Hills."

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Lafe Lingers On" - Lafe Crick comes back in another scheme to get rich off the Clampetts' wealth.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Girl From Home" - A father and daughter from back home come to Beverly Hills to meet Jed.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Giant Jackrabbit" - Granny encounters a kangaroo, and thinks she's found a giant jackrabbit, but no one will believe her.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: Mr. Drysdale's idea for Christmas presents are ones only fit for coastal California, like diving suits and a boat, and a television set, which Granny thinks is some kind of fancy washing machine.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Get Culture" - The Clampetts decide to get a taste of culture to fit in the high-class society of Beverly Hills.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Elly Needs a Maw" - Mr. Drysdale needs investors for his real estate development project, so he enlists the Widow Fenwick, and aims to cement the deal by hitching her to Jed Clampett, who is looking to find a mother for his daughter, Elly May.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Garden Party" - Mrs. Drysdale has a garden party and 'invites' the Clampetts by mistake. They end up back by their 'cement pond' to handle the 'overflow' from Mrs. Drysdale's party. A gun shot by Granny starts the real party.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "Turkey Day" - Elly bonds with the Thanksgiving turkey so the family can't bring themselves to kill it; Mrs Drysdale hires some Indians to take a Thanksgiving picture, which puts Granny on the warpath.

programme.title: The Beverly Hillbillies
programme.desc: "The Clampetts Go Hollywood" - When Jake Clampett tells Jed he's his cousin, he's welcomed into the home and soon has everyone but Jed thinking about and dressing for a role in their next movie.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Lucas becomes involved in a battle between an aspiring writer and a cocky, bragging trail boss.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Mark runs away from home after his father questions his story about overhearing a plot to rob the bank.

programme.desc: A famous buffalo hunter tries to goad Lucas into a shooting match. When Lucas refuses, he becomes the target.

programme.desc: Mark finds himself in grave danger when two unscrupulous detectives searching for a man's long-lost son claim that it's Mark.

programme.desc: Lucas and Mark find themselves held at gunpoint by a prisoner being transported on a train, which becomes stranded from a plague of grasshoppers.

programme.desc: Lucas and Marshall Torrance get into trouble with the Army when they try to help a wounded young soldier who deserted.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: After Mark contracts typhoid fever and has a vision of his dead mother he no longer seems interested in getting well.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Several men are vying for a prosperous rancher's daughter's affections. When she is found dead, one of them becomes the prime suspect in her murder.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Lucas receives a series of mysterious threatening notes from an ex-convict with a grudge.

programme.title: The Phantom Stagecoach
programme.desc: An undercover Wells Fargo agent discovers robberies plaguing a stagecoach are the work of a rival company.

programme.title: Buchanan Rides Alone
programme.desc: En route back to Texas, Buchanan rides into a California town and becomes embroiled in a violent family fued.

programme.title: The Parson And The Outlaw
programme.desc: Dexter (fka Billy the Kid) hangs up his guns in an attempt to forget the past and start a new life.

programme.title: The Kid From Broken Gun
programme.desc: Told in flashback, Mahoney is charged with murder and Starrett proves to his lawyer that Stevens is really the guilty party.

programme.title: Montana Territory
programme.desc: An idealistic deputy is disillusioned to learn that a sheriff he idolizes is using his badge as a cover for his crimes.

programme.title: Blackjack Ketchum, Desperado
programme.desc: A legendary gunslinger joins his fiancee in outlaw-ruled Oxhorn, hoping to live down his deadly reputation.

programme.title: The Deadly Companions
programme.desc: An unholy alliance! Marking the feature film debut of one of Hollywoods most influential and widely referenced auteur directors, Sam Peckinpah, The Deadly Companions centers on a redemptive journey to escort a funeral procession through dangerous Indian Territory. Featuring fine work from a top-notch cast that includes the inimitable Maureen OHara at the height of her illustrious career, Peckinpahs first foray into film provides a promising glance into the energetic and violent nature of his iconic later works.

programme.title: Last of the Clintons
programme.desc: Icon of early talking pictures, Harry Carey (Red River), portrays the rugged Trigger Carson, a noble cowboy who infiltrates an outlaw gang in order to expose their rackets. Ordered to kidnap a young girl, his identity is revealed and he is forced to fight his way out of the gang and back to safety. Already 56 by the time he signed on to do this and a series of Westerns for Berke Productions, the stately Carrey (who netted an Oscar nomination four years later) was widely lauded at the time for adding a great deal of maturity and respectability due to his undeniable presence and strong performances.

programme.title: Neath Canadian Skies
programme.desc: It's the mounties versus claim jumpers in this drama from Golden Gate Pictures. Based on an action-packed novel by James Oliver Curwood and directed by B. Reeves Maynard (Undersea Kingdom, The Phantom Empire), the film features an outstanding cast of Poverty Row regulars, including Russell Hayden, Inez Cooper, Cliff Nazarro, I. Stanford Jolley, and Kermit Maynard. For movie buffs: Cinematographer Marcel Le Picard began his career in the silent era and finished with exactly 200 credits!

programme.title: Red Ryder: Ride, Ryder, Ride!
programme.desc: Red Ryder encounters bandits trying to hold up the stagecoach carrying the owner of the Devil's Hole newspaper, Libby Brooks and her brother Gerry. Red successfully drives off the attackers but becomes embroiled in further conflict, leading to a duel that nearly claims his life.

programme.title: Fighting Fury
programme.desc: A THRILLING TALE OF REVENGE AND REDEMPTION! Based on the story Triple Cross for Danger by the widely lauded Western author Walter J. Coburn, Fighting Fury is a thrilling take on the Western revenge drama. Portraying both a father and his son, star of silent and sound cowboy adventure films, Jack Hoxie (Trouble Busters, Law and Lawless) exudes the classic American rustic charm that still defines the genre. The film spins the yarn of Clay Hill Jr. (Hoxie), a Spanish-American boy raised by a Mexican servant who vows bloody vengeance on three malicious ranchers after they murder his parents and turn his life upside down. Featuring fine supporting performances from genre heavies Helen Holmes, Buck Conners and Western mainstay Fred Kohler.

programme.title: Abilene Town
programme.desc: Ablaze with guns and guts and glory! Randolph Scott headlines as a star studded cast in this yarn about battling homesteaders. Director Edwin L. Marin (Tall in the Saddle) spins this dramatic post-Civil War tale that takes place in the legendary cow town of Abilene, Kansas, coaxing fine performances from the likes of Ann Dvorak, Edgar Buchanan, Thonda Fleming, and Lloyd Bridges. Saddle up and hang on!

programme.title: Shine on Harvest Moon
programme.desc: Jackson and his gang are cattle rustlers holed up in Jackson Hole Wyoming. Roy's partner Brower was once Jackson's partner and is now suspected of being in cahoots with him. Someone is tipping off Jackson as to the cattle's whereabouts and when Roy learns who it is he has a plan to trap Jackson and clear his partner. Starring Roy Rogers, Lynne Roberts, and Myrtle Wiseman as 'Lulu Belle. Directed by Joseph Kane.

programme.title: The Grand Duel
programme.desc: An ex-sheriff helps protect a man framed for murder from brothers who want him dead.

programme.title: Doc Holliday 2
programme.desc: After the OK Coral shootout in Tombstone, Doc Holliday opens a small Faro Saloon in a remote town where he encounters Edward Bailey. When a Faro game goes wrong for Edward, he turns up missing and Doc Holliday finds himself on trial for his murder.

programme.title: Andromeda: 1883

programme.title: The Undefeated
programme.desc: Unaware that the Civil War has ended, Col. John Henry Thomas (John Wayne) successfully attacks a Confederate company led by James Langdon (Rock Hudson). With the remnants of Langdon's squadron fleeing to Mexico, Thomas and his adopted Native American son, Blue Boy (Roman Gabriel), gather a herd of horses and head for the border with their company, to sell them. After Langdon's caravan is captured by a Mexican general (Tony Aguilar), Thomas nobly sacrifices the horses to free them.

programme.title: Duct Tape Forever
programme.desc: When an evil land baron's limo falls into a sinkhole outside Possum Lodge, a judge orders the men to pay $10,000 in damages in 10 days or lose their beloved lodge forever. Lodge leader Red Green considers various money-making schemes the men dream up, like selling road kill on the Internet. But it's Harold, Red's nerdy nephew, who comes up with an idea that just might save their wood-smoked bacon: a duct tape sculpture contest in Minneapolis with a $10,000 third-place prize. Before you can say "go for the bronze," Red and Harold are hitching the Possum van to their can't-fail, low-ball entry and heading for trouble. Will Harold get the credit he craves and the girl he deserves (or vice versa)? Is their goose cooked or will it fly?

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Valentine's Day week launches with Carla Hall making an Easy Chicken Curry that she considers the perfect romantic dinner: warming, aromatic, and impressive without being stressful. The hosts debate whether cooking for a date should feel effortless or theatrical and share their own most romantic food memories.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly makes his legendary Sexy Time Chicken for Valentine's Day, a dish he swears exudes confidence, intention, and genuine romance with every bite. Guest Devyn Simone joins to share relationship etiquette wisdom, and the hosts explore what makes food truly feel romantic beyond just the recipe itself.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Amanda Freitag joins Clinton Kelly, Carla Hall, and Michael Symon for the Valentine's Day week finale, making a romantic pasta dish that inspires Michael to quote an Italian chef who calls the vigorous mixing of pasta 'the sound of love.' The hosts celebrate food as the ultimate love language and toast to the week with warmth, humor, and fresh pasta.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly arrives furious after producer Nick bans his gorgeous Ted Baker sweater due to moirÈ camera interference, and the ensuing sweater feud becomes the running gag of the episode as Chewed Up enters an Anything Goes week with no cooking theme and maximum creative freedom. Michael and Carla enjoy Clinton's frustration immensely.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly pitches his inspired Loaf Three Ways concept for Anything Goes week, proposing that each host make a different loaf ó savory, sweet, and somewhere in between ó in a format he has been developing for months. The hosts celebrate the Indiana Jones Temple of Doom opening number as the perfect anthem for a week with no rules.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly replaces cooking with a March Madness-style bracket tournament for boxed brownie mixes, staging eight brands in head-to-head blind taste tests to crown an ultimate winner. After an epic series of eliminations, Duncan Hines Double Fudge emerges victorious at just $4.29 a box.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Jackie Bakula, beloved as Sparkles to Chewed Up fans, returns to the kitchen as a guest host while Carla Hall is away filming the Today Show, bringing her warm, professional energy and joining Michael Symon and Clinton Kelly for a celebratory cooking episode full of affection for the show.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Korean-American chef Esther Choi joins for Meat Optional Week, making Kimchi Fried Rice that converts even committed carnivores, while Clinton Kelly prepares his beloved home-alone tofu dish and the hosts celebrate the surprising depth and satisfaction of meatless cooking.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall closes out Meat Optional Week with a bold Butternut Squash Chili served over golden rounds of pre-made polenta, proving that a vegetarian chili can be just as hearty and craveable as any meat-based version. The hosts discuss the magic of pot liquor, winter herb gardening, and the underappreciated power of cooking tomatoes long enough to balance their acidity.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Michael Symon opens Home Alone Week by making his ideal personal omelet ó the dish he truly loves cooking just for himself ó as the hosts celebrate an entire week of cooking without compromise, audience, or spousal interference. The conversation digs into the authentic food preferences everyone quietly keeps to themselves.

programme.title: Chewed Up
programme.desc: Carla Hall stuns Michael and Clinton by revealing that her perfect home-alone meal is chili dogs ó a dish the Michelin-adjacent chef makes entirely for herself when no one is watching. The hosts celebrate the week's theme that authentic food preferences are often the most surprising and humanizing.

programme.title: What To Cook Next
programme.desc: Filipino breakfast staples, explained. Nico cooks tortang talong, longsilog, and garlic rice — comfort food that anchors the day with savory, soulful flavors.

programme.title: What To Cook Next
programme.desc: Nico prepares kinilaw with scallop (or oyster) and a bright mango-tomato salad, showing how acid, freshness, and restraint define Filipino raw dishes.

programme.title: Behind the Counter with David Chang
programme.desc: Behind the Counter - The Vegan Korean Menu: This week, Dave is developing a vegan Korean menu for guests Nikki Glaser and Pete Holmes. Dishes include: Banchan, the Korean name for side dishes served with rice; fried mushroom Tangsuyuk; crispy bindaetteok cones; avocado rice; kongguksu; and Asian pear shaved ice.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave makes a quick snack that you might see more and more often in the coming years: dried squid. He talks about the rising amount of cephalopods in the ocean, the specific umami and texture of squid, and ponders if it might be coming to more and more grocery store shelves near you. He also answers an Ask Dave about London eating, and general dining strategy when on a short trip in a new place.

programme.title: The Dave Chang Show
programme.desc: Dave has Marc Johnson and Andrew Miller, friends of the show and culinary producers of Dinner Time Live, on the podcast as they test a dish for the 50th episode of Dinner Time Live. They test a poulet en vessie in a variety of forms, one of which may or may not have made it to the real episode on Netflix. While they test pig bladders and chicken, they talk about their favorite dishes from 50 episodes and 3 seasons of Dinner Time Live.

programme.title: Behind the Counter with David Chang
programme.desc: Behind the Counter with David Chang - Classic Comforts: An unfiltered behind-the-scenes look into David Chang's kitchen as he and his team develop and practice a unique menu in the days leading up to the next Netflix episode of Dinner Time Live. This episode sees Dave working on a "Classic Comfort Foods" menu for NBA legends Reggie Miller and Mark Jackson. Dishes include: Caesar salad, New England clam chowder, fettuccine alfredo, salmon with caper-butter sauce, a potato rösti, and a chocolate tart.

programme.title: Behind the Counter with David Chang
programme.desc: Behind the Counter with David Chang - Fish on the Menu: An unfiltered behind-the-scenes look into David Chang's kitchen as he and his team develop and practice a unique menu in the days leading up to the next Netflix episode of Dinner Time Live. In this episode, Dave is creating a "Fish on the Menu" theme for Emily in Paris stars Lily Collins and Ashley Park. Dishes include: Hwe (two ways), Uni & Chickpeas, an Ode to Noma's Langoustine, Roasted Flounder, Fish Soup with Rice and Pickles, and Flottante.

programme.title: Recipe Club Recipes

Make this, pop it in the freezer, give yourself a whipped cream shot straight from the can, and bask in the accolades you receive from anyone you choose to share with.

programme.title: Pati's Mexican Table
programme.desc: In Casas Grandes, Pati rides in style with local art curator Mayte Luján in her 1960s Cadillac. Mayte owns a bed and breakfast, Las Guacamayas, where she invites Pati into the kitchen to make chile con queso in impossibly soft flour tortillas. Later, Pati learns about another tasty export from Chihuahua, pecans, at Gustavo Vázquez's farm, where his family has been growing them for generations.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Barbecue on a Budget: A lot of American barbecue began with inexpensive meat cuts, like spareribs, beef shoulder, and pork belly. With the economic insecurities brought on by COVID- 9, we’re all feeling the pinch, and budget grilling has taken on new urgency. Besides, why should Kobe beef and tomahawk steaks get all the love? This is barbecue on a budget.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Sustainable Seafood: Pollution. Overfishing. Abusive labor practices. There’s a lot of negative news coming out about the seafood industry lately, and much of it breaks your heart. Today’s show celebrates seafood we can eat because it’s fished in a way that’s humane and environmentally sound. Activists call it sustainable seafood. I call it three great reasons for firing up your grill.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Social Distance Tailgating: From its humble origins on the back of a buckboard wagon to today’s high-octane outdoor foodie extravaganzas, tailgating continues to enthrall—make that obsess—American sports fans. This episode is our our annual celebration of tailgating with a global twist. The socializing may be distant this year—all the more reason to up your game at the grill.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Little Italy: When I was growing up, a trip to Baltimore’s Little Italy always meant culinary adventure. Today we celebrate Italian grilling and the food of Little Italys around North America, honoring the Italian reverence for simplicity and fresh seasonal ingredients, and the American passion for big flavors and ingenious grilling techniques.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Killer Barbecue – Hold the Meat You don’t need a degree in barbecue to know that vegetables have hit the grill big time. Veggies for hardcore carnivores who crave killer accompaniments to their meats, for health-conscious grillers, and for everyone who delights in the smokiness and supernatural sweetness live fire imparts to plant and dairy foods.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Charm City ‘Que: Baltimore, the town where I grew up, boasts plenty of awesome foods for grilling. Pit beef was born here. In the seafood department you find a sweet white-fleshed fish the rest of the world calls striped bass and Baltimoreans know as rockfish. And no Charm City meal would be complete without chocolate top cookies.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Barbecue on a Budget: A lot of American barbecue began with inexpensive meat cuts, like spareribs, beef shoulder, and pork belly. With the economic insecurities brought on by COVID- 9, we’re all feeling the pinch, and budget grilling has taken on new urgency. Besides, why should Kobe beef and tomahawk steaks get all the love? This is barbecue on a budget.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Sustainable Seafood: Pollution. Overfishing. Abusive labor practices. There’s a lot of negative news coming out about the seafood industry lately, and much of it breaks your heart. Today’s show celebrates seafood we can eat because it’s fished in a way that’s humane and environmentally sound. Activists call it sustainable seafood. I call it three great reasons for firing up your grill.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Cured and Smoked: In humankind’s long march to food security, two ancient preserving techniques have stood out over the millennia: curing and smoking. The first involves preserving foods with salt, soy sauce, or sodium nitrite, and the second technique involves blasting foods with flavorful clouds of wood smoke. Today, we’re pushing the envelope on traditional curing and smoking.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Maryland Crab Feast: This show focuses on my favorite food growing up: callinectes sapidus, better known as the Maryland blue crab. Maryland seafood markets and restaurants come alive with soft shell crabs, eaten shell and all, traditionally deep-fried, here seared over live fire. Which brings us to the summum of the Maryland crab experience: a crab feast known as Maryland steamed crabs.

programme.title: Steven Raichlen's Project Fire
programme.desc: Steak. Few other words in the English language have such power to make our hearts beat faster and our mouths water than sizzling slabs of beef seared over live fire. In this show we embark on some meaty globe hopping, exploring over-the-top steaks from France, Cambodia, and the American Southwest. Hot fire. Sizzling meat. It’s a carnivore’s dream come true.

programme.title: A Taste of History
programme.desc: Set sail with Chef Walter Staib on a two-part adventure down the majestic Danube River! Part 1 charts a course through Germany and Austria, exploring stunning valleys amidst Europe's heart. Discover historic sites, artistic treasures, and culinary delights at every stop along this unforgettable journey.

programme.title: A Taste of History
programme.desc: Our journey continues through Austria, Slovakia, and ending in the Hungarian city of Budapest. We’ll marvel at architectural wonders and savor the flavors of the region with Chef Walter Staib as he prepares traditional meals aboard a luxurious cruise ship. Don't miss this unforgettable finale on the mighty Danube!

programme.title: A Taste of History
programme.desc: Journey to St. John's, Newfoundland, to discover the deep connection locals have with nature's bounty, and meet inspiring individuals who fish, farm, and cook with generations of tradition. Savor unique recipes prepared over crackling fire-pits, including the king of St. John's cuisine: cod.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: The O'Kane family's patience is truly tested during a complete to-the-studs kitchen renovation. Homeowner Yvonne starts to get impatient to feed her family home cooked dinners. Added pressure: another fireplace to deal with, uneven floors, custom cabinets and an uncovered secret staircase. (All that plus a new puppy.) Thanks to Mar's 6 design principles this custom space is unique and stunning.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: It's 'Lost in Space' because somehow these home makeover clips never made it to Mar's! Bloopers Alert! Unseen Tips Alert! Inspired by viewer feedback, an entire show that includes highlights of the greatest gaffs, goofs and guffaws. Plus, some renovation and makeover projects never before aired.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Mar Jennings uses his 6 Casual Luxury design principles to transform a typical master bedroom, closets and bath into a boutique, hotel-style oasis. The O'Kanes are desperate to stop sleeping on their couches and get back into their master bedroom - but with a new cathedral ceiling installation, private deck installation, custom vanities, several setbacks and delays this renovation won't be easy.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Not only does Mar Jennings have the task of pleasing the adult O'Kanes, but he has to satisfy Teddy, a young man between childhood and young adulthood. AND try to make space in an already-small bedroom. Can it even be done? All while juggling two desk and office spaces-for Teddy and his parents. Mar's unexpected solutions for this kids bedroom and office makeover are truly Casual Luxury.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: More things that regular episodes just couldn't fit in, so we say they didn't quite make it to Mar's! Filled with some of Mar's favorite people and places that viewers wanted to see more of, and of course some delightfully hilarious bloopers, too. Plus, more renovation, makeover and design sMARt tips (like fixing uneven floors) and great locations. As always, Casual Luxury has no boundaries.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Mar is tasked with carving out two new basement rooms from storage space: a studio and laundry room. With Mar's 6 design principles, he must make dark and dank become bright and stunning. The homeowners expect to be wowed by these 'creative' and 'clean' space makeovers, but with pipes and beams in the way and unexpected challenges, this project might just push Mar over the edge.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Homeowners Neil & Yvonne challenge Mar to makeover a pool area-adjacent media room and make it all-season functional for entertaining. Mar uses his 6 exclusive Casual Luxury design principles to completely re-think the area, including reclaiming basement space to add a bonus kitchenette & full bath. Walls and a fireplace must go! Will the family be ready to entertain-and will they love the reveal?

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Witness the renovation evolution of some truly reMARkable outdoor living spaces: new pergola, new pool deck and new sun deck. (You won't believe the magical makeover transformation of the garage roof!) And Mar gets a visit from a TV friend, who he sneaks in for peek at the progress before the family gets their final look at the space.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Can Mar handle a surprise project? You bet. Follow this unplanned-for renovation and design overhaul of the main floor family room and living room. Mar's hope for the reveal? That the O'Kane family will love the relaxed feeling of the family room and how he's upping the glamour quotient of the living room. Meanwhile, a secret time capsule sheds light on the home's history.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Watch as the landscape makeover design takes shape, going from overgrown and out of place to perfectly balanced, for the ultimate Casual Luxury curb appeal. Sometimes, the design process hurts. Mar needs the homeowner, Yvonne, to put nostalgic connections aside in order to handle the extensive renovations required.

programme.title: Life on Mar's: The Home Makeover Show
programme.desc: Time to gut and renovate two out-of-date and well-used kids' bathrooms! As payback for being displaced, the 3 kids 'treat' Mar to the Trapeze School of New York-he responds with a trip to Six Flags new England. (Cue the screaming.) Meanwhile, the transformational Casual Luxury bathroom makeovers are taking shape. After only a few surprises and challenges, this reveal is the kids' favorite one yet.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: This project got started months ago—but this mom didn’t know where to go to finish it. See how we turn a previously unusable space into a cozy nook for her daughter.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: When your house has the right size, shape and location, but not the right character for your family, it’s time for a rustic renovation.

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford

programme.title: Today's Homeowner with Danny Lipford
programme.desc: The bugs make it nearly impossible for this family to use their covered back porch. This week, we’re screening it in to make it more useful all year round.

programme.title: Music Voyager
programme.desc: Venture along the Yellow Roads of Italy. We begin with a private performance at the Posta de Donini Hotel, followed by a visit to the city of Perugia. Also, dine in the lush vineyards of Lungarotti, and get a private tour of the Vatican.

programme.title: Smart Travels with Rudy Maxa
programme.desc: We wend our way across this largest island in the Mediterranean Sea. Once the hub of the ancient world, Sicily offers up a vibrant, sun-soaked brew of history, tradition, and intense cultural pride. A climb up Mount Etna for its unearthly views will delight.

programme.title: Smart Travels with Rudy Maxa
programme.desc: On the Greek Islands we get familiar with the ferries as we island-hop the Aegean. Sun-drenched, upscale, and exotic, Santorini sits on the rim of an ancient, still active, volcano. At one end of the island, the archaeological site at Akrotiri uncovers a city frozen in time by an ancient eruption.

programme.title: Smart Travels with Rudy Maxa
programme.desc: Deep in the heart of Naples, Italy is the ideal launching point for exploring Italy's great historic treasures. Marvel at the ancient city of Pompeii, and the eerily captured devastation of Mount Vesuvius. Hop a ferry to the isle of Capri and drive the spectacular Amalfi Coast.

programme.title: Porthole Cruise and Travel Show
programme.desc: Set sail and cruise to the Mediterranean's top destinations in France and Italy.

programme.title: Traveling with Denella Ri'chard
programme.desc: Mediterranean with Denella Ri'chard.

programme.title: My Greek Table with Diane Kochilas
programme.desc: Lesvos is among the most vibrant and fascinating Greek islands, where the crossroads of history is exemplified by local dishes featuring the sea's bounty, eggplant, and Ouzo. Diane gets an insider's taste with a good friend and local son.

programme.title: Dream of Italy
programme.desc: We begin with a hike to find white truffles before attending the World Truffle Auction. We will also visit Cascina Bruciata vineyard, Lago delle Sorgenti for some deep relaxation, as well as Lake Iseo to witness fine Italian boat making.

programme.title: Dream of Italy
programme.desc: Author Frances Mayes and her husband Ed join Kathy in Cortona, Tuscany for a delightful visit that includes a rare tour of the couple's exquisite home, Villa Bramasole. Artisan, artistic, and culinary treasures await under the Tuscan sun.

programme.title: Dream of Italy
programme.desc: There is only one Venice. Join Kathy as she receives rowing lessons from an 8th-generation gondolier and learns how Venice is contending with rising sea levels. We will also meet an impiraressa and visit the ancient Rialto Market.

programme.title: Traveling with Denella Ri'chard

programme.title: Smart Travels with Rudy Maxa

programme.title: Smart Travels with Rudy Maxa
programme.desc: We leave the eternal city behind by way of the famous Appian Way to explore the environs of Rome. First it's the majesty of Emperor Hadrian's villa and the wine and lakes of the Alban Hills. Next, it's south to the ancient seaport of Ostia, Rome's Pompeii.

programme.title: Smart Travels with Rudy Maxa
programme.desc: We are off to the Italian Riviera seeking la dolce vita in one of the world's most romantic locations. With awe-inspiring cliffs, and beaches that shimmer like jewels on the Mediterranean, the Riviera is as stunning a coastline as anywhere in Europe.

programme.title: Scenes from a Creek

programme.title: Yosemite Magic

programme.title: Underwater Paradise

programme.title: The World's Most Beautiful Places

programme.title: Finny's First Day Of School - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Finny is a bit nervous on the first day of school, but Myrtle the Turtle is even more anxious and literally hides her shell! Surely Myrtle will come out of her shell when their teacher, a whale named Mr. Magellan, has them draw and present their favorite things to the class! Nope. Myrtle is even more nervous than ever! Being a good friend, Finny helps Myrtle through her anxiety, and the tiny turtle finally shows her drawing to everyone.

programme.title: Baby Shark - Finny The Shark and more
programme.desc: Baby Shark, Mama Shark, Papa Shark, Grandma Shark, and Grandpa Shark are out for a family swim and Baby Shark tries and tries to catch the little fishies but his skills aren’t yet up to the task. Just when he thinks he finally has them a (very big) friend of the little fishies steps in to save the day!

programme.title: Dharma's Digger - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Oh no! While digging deep at a top secret construction site, Dhama’s digger gets splashed with smelly sludge! Everyone says that Carl's Car Wash is the place to get all cleaned up, so Dharma skedaddles over to see how Carl can help. With a quick mess assessment and a few twists of some knobs, Carl’s suds-sational car wash is ready to give Dharma’s vehicle a good scrub-a-dub! Dharma rolls out one happy customer, and ready to get back to work on the site that Carl is itching to see!

programme.title: Mr. Golden Sun - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Mister Sun, Sun, Mr. Golden Sun...please shine down on me! It’s the Mr. Sun song from Super Simple Songs!

programme.title: Finny's First Sleepover - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: It’s Finny’s first sleepover and Finny is nervous to sleep at Myrtle the Turtle’s house. Throughout the night, Finny becomes more and more comfortable and after a spooky story, Finny becomes the bravest of everyone at the sleepover! Finny can't wait to do a sleepover again – but this time, at Finny’s house!

programme.title: When I Grow Up - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: What do you want to be when you grow up? Maybe a firefighter? Or an astronaut? Or maybe a dancer? Practice talking about jobs and careers with this super simple and super fun song, “When I Grow Up”.

programme.title: Enchanted Ice Cream - Super Simple
programme.desc: Today, the Bumble Nums are going to make Enchanted Ice Cream Sundaes! With a map in hand, Humble, Grumble, and Stumble search the orchard for the secret ingredient … and discover a fairy treehouse leading to an enchanted world! The Bumble Nums travel through the portal…but do they find the ingredient they’re looking for? And can they get it back to the kitchen in time for the Cooking Countdown? Watch and see!

programme.title: Hello Hello! - Super Simple Puppets and more
programme.desc: Sing and dance along with the Super Simple Puppets to “Hello Hello!”! It’s a classic Super Simple action song for kids.

programme.title: Down By The Bay - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Did you ever see cat wearing a hat? How about a goat rowing a boat? Well you can in this video for the classic children’s song, Down By The Bay!

programme.title: Erupting Raspberry Lava Cake - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: The Bumble Nums are making an Erupting Raspberry Lava Cake but the secret ingredient is at the top of Mount Fruity Volcano! Can Humble, Grumble, and Stumble climb the volcano and get what they need before it blows? And can they do it in time for the Cooking Countdown? Watch and see!

programme.title: Finny The Shark - Down In The Bay and more
programme.desc: Did you ever see a shark glowing in the dark? Did you ever see a stingray dancing at the ballet? Finny and Mama Shark are singing a rhyming song about all of their silly friends down in the bay! Sing along!

programme.title: Let's Go For A Drive! - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Get in on the fun and join Milo in his pretend car for a quick ride. Do you see the ducks crossing the road? How about people walking on the sidewalk? Wait, don't cross on a red light!

programme.title: Mr. Pig & Mr. Mouse Have A Mess On Their Hands - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Mr. Mouse and Mr. Pig love making food and having their friends over to eat it. But afterwards, their house is a complete mess! It's so gross, they don't even want to drive their van home to clean up. How can Mr. Mouse and Mr. Pig share their love of food with their friends without cluttering their home? Mr. Monkey has a tasty idea! What is it? Watch and find out!

programme.title: The Pinocchio - Sing Along With Tobee - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: Caitie made a fluffy bird marionette named Fifi! Fifi is full of jokes and Tobee can't stop laughing! Caitie knows another marionette who is just as silly as Fifi. It's Pinocchio! There's even a whole song about him called "The Pinocchio." After Caitie and Tobee sing and dance to the song, Fifi can't help but tell one last joke. And guess what? It quacks Tobee up!

programme.title: 10 Little Dinosaurs - Super Simple and more
programme.desc: 10 Stegosauruses! 10 Triceratopses! 10 Pterodactyls! 10 Brontosauruses! And 10 Tyrannosaurus Rex! Wow! This Super Simple counting song introduces the numbers 1-10 and also some very cool dinosaur names! Can you say them all?!

programme.title: Little Billy's Skateboard - Carl's Car Wash and more
programme.desc: The Bubblegum Bandit pulls another bubblegum burglary and is about to escape when Officer Oona bursts his bubble! Moments later, little Billy skates right into the action and his skateboard becomes a gummy mess! Billy hurries to Carl’s Car Wash, but Carl won't clean his skateboard - it's a CAR wash, not a SKATEBOARD wash! Billy does some quick thinking...and his skateboard might just go through the car wash after all!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett delivers winter clothes to a little girl living in Egypt's Sahara Desert where a cold snap has left the desert snowy. Jett and Astra show her how to enjoy the snow. After the Super Pets accidentally knock down some Turkish rugs in an Istanbul market, Jett and Ellie deliver a magic lamp that can summon two genies.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: A boy, Kima, and his Dad are trekking through the Himalaya mountains in Nepal. The Super Wings bring an LED torch to help them. Trouble strikes when a baby yeti startles them. Vo, the Mars rover, is celebrating his birthday but misses his friend Catalina. Jett and Astra come to the rescue and promise to bring Catalina and her ginormous gift to Mars.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: For Children's Day, Wang Min's Dad has invited his son and his classmates to visit his toy factory. While cleaning up before the visit, a robot toy goes on a rampage. The Super Pets encounter an itchy lion at a sanctuary in South Africa. Jett and Astra deliver a back scratcher, but the lion's itchiness is stronger than they realized!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets go into a video game arcade and play a video game called Dessert Kingdom. They are watched by a boy named Yoonho and they decide to go into the game itself. When the Super Pets unearth a woolly mammoth, a Nenets boy wants to keep it. The mammoth must be able to pull a sleigh so Jett and Dizzy deliver a mammoth size sleigh.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: When a T-Rex skeleton on display in a Copenhagen museum is brought to life, a boy orders a remote controller to summon a giant robot T-Rex that he built to help calm it. Jett and the others arrive in the Jurassic Period to return Tyranno to her proper place in time. A volcano erupts throwing fireballs everywhere and causing a dinosaur stampede.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets spend a day at a Pet Hotel in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. The daughter of the hotel owner gets help from the Super Wings to keep them well behaved. After the Jet Pets' Mini Base collides with a small comet, Jett and Astra rush a space scanner to a young boy and together and the three search space for the small 'Baba' comet.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: It's Jett's birthday and almost all of his friends are throwing him a big birthday party! Sky sends Donnie, Paul, and Dizzy out on missions and nobody is left at Jett's party. When the truck transporting race cars gets lost, Jett, Jerome and Dizzy show up in Monza, Italy to take part in a very special Super Wings race against Golden Boy.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: While in Thailand, Jett Pet and Paul Pet's fruit is swiped by a monkey. Thanawat asks for help getting his toys back so they create an LED ball with a tracking system. The Super Pets travel to Madrid, Spain where they help a boy secure a case for his pet tarantula to ride the train. However an accident causes his tarantula to become super-sized!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: After eating Super Pets' trick chocolates, Jett can only move very slowly. An emergency call comes in from a young girl in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, who needs an ice maker delivered. When the Super Pets discover an erupting underwater volcano, a girl orders a megaphone to alert all the villagers fishing in the water. However, they don't listen to her warnings.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets are on World Aircraft to help Jett give some kids a tour. After Storm demonstrates how the package printer works, two packages get mixed up. The Super Pets travel to Tokyo, Japan where they help the Paper Rangers repair an Origami Robot for an action movie. Afterward, they get asked to be ACTORS in the movie!

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets encounter mermaid students learning to befriend fish in Cebu, Philippines. Jett and Jerome deliver a microphone and speaker to a girl who wants to sing with them. The Super Pets meet a girl in Paris whose greatest wish is to go to the top of the Eiffel Tower. The elevator cannot run due to an expected storm so Jett and Donnie deliver a tram.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Storm and Stella search for Jett and Donnie after the two Super Wings disappear in thin air. Astra locates their whereabouts - they're in Paris, 1889! While exploring Osaka, Japan, the Super Pets accidentally rip a boy's koinobori for the Children's Day Festival. Jett and Dizzy deliver a huge koinobori for him.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: The Super Pets meet a boy and his giant robot on Jeju Island in South Korea. Before a typhoon comes, Taehoon needs to help his father harvest all the tangerines on their farm. When a little girl and her Dad accidentally fall in an Incan secret dark room, Jett, and Ellie show up in Machu Picchu, Peru, to take part in a historical rescue mission.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: When the Super Pets arrive at Ha Long, Vietnam, they meet a boy who wants to take his pet snail on a walk but the snail is too slow. So the boy asks for a snail size skateboard. The Super Pets go to Germany where they participate in a water fight. They accidentally shoot Mona's necklace. Jett and Astra deliver a magnifying glass to find the missing bead.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: When the Super Pets accidentally break a boulder on the roof of a house in Monsanto, Portugal, a girl orders tape to stick it back together. Golden Boy tries to outshine them. The Super Pets' Mini Base accidentally gets tangled up in an octopus kite, pulling it to the ground and destroying it. The Super Wings deliver paper and string to make a new kite.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Marty, with their new electric powers, save an amusement park in Germany. Jett and Dizzy also rescue a boat, an eel, and fix a jaguar chase, making everyone happy.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: In Singapore, Jett and Lucie use electric powers to shrink Happy the giant robot dog. In Paris, Jett and Marty turn a giant baguette into a train to deliver it to a festival.

programme.title: Super Wings
programme.desc: Jett and Traver time-travel to uncover a dinosaur footprint’s creator. Jett and Dizzy rescue a bus stuck in a ditch using Monster Truck Tires, getting the kids to school on time.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: From the moment babies are born, they use their developing senses to discover the world. In terms of sight, bold patterns in contrasting colors or black and white are initially preferred. As for sounds, Mother s gentle voice, in any language, is music to baby's ears. Language Nursery: Voices From Many Lands acquaints your baby with the sounds of foreign language by presenting delightful, visually stimulating images accompanied by spoken passages, natural sounds and music.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Dive in with your little explorer across the seas to meet amazing animals living in the ocean, coral reef, and polar regions. Overflowing with underwater footage of whales, dolphins, tropical fish, and more, Neptune's Oceans invites you and your baby on an oceanic voyage of discovery!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: World Music introduces you and your little one to beautiful sounds from every continent, bringing a fresh, new experience of music and culture. Sing, dance and play to exotic rhythms that open the door to an appreciation of different cultures from around the world.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Baby Lullaby takes your baby on a soothing journey with classical lullabies by Bach, Brahms and Mozart. Enjoy relaxing ocean waves, sweet puppet shows and tranquil images. From fun time to rest time, Baby Lullaby is the perfect way to spend special time with your baby.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Discover the exciting world of shapes and colors! Learn about the primary colors red, yellow, and blue with real-life imagery of animals and objects. Explore colors like purple, orange, and green and see artwork by Van Gogh! Discover the circles, squares, and triangles that surround us in everyday life, and introduce baby to the world of visual arts as children and artists create their own work!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Ocean Explorers
programme.desc: Cal, Neptune, Opus, Dean, Tinker and others learn about the flora and fauna of the ocean through STEAM-based principles and real-life footage.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Join our playful puppet host, Bard the Dragon, on a fun-filled adventure using everyday words such as flower, cat, and moon, and beautiful scenery from nature. World Of Words lets you and your little one share the excitement of discovering new words together!

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Perhaps the sweetest sounds you'll ever hear are the babbles and coos that one day become ma-ma, da-da and I love you. Besides being music to your ears, these baby steps of language change everything as your little one discovers an exciting, powerful new way of relating to the world! Baby's First Sounds invites both of you to share these memorable moments: those precious ''firsts'' that come around once in your baby's lifetime.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: With the Dolittle, and other Baby programs under their belts, the tots who follow Julie Aigner-Clark's Baby Einstein series will sidle up to Baby Newton expecting a similar exploration of building blocks to brilliance, but this time there's a wrinkle or two. Animation makes its first appearance, and parents steeped in keep-it-simple culture may object to the springy "I Know My Shapes" song, a departure from classical-only video predecessors.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: As little ones grow, their innate sense of wonder grows too, creating an occasion for celebration with each new discovery. Baby Galileo Discovering the Sky introduces them to that remarkable canopy above us: the sun and moon, fluffy clouds, shimmering stars, colorful planets and whirling galaxies.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Within a baby's first year of life, new experiences can transform what might otherwise seem to be ordinary events into exciting opportunities for imaginative play. Baby Neptune exposes little ones to the wonders of water in their world -- whether they're stomping in the rain, splashing in the bathtub, playing "catch me if you can" with the tide on the beach, or "meeting" the fascinating creatures that live in rivers, lakes and oceans.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: Sharing the joys and traditions of the holiday season with your little one is truly one of the greatest gifts of parenthood, and Baby Santa's Music Box makes this magical time even more special! Celebrate by treating your baby and yourself to a captivating wonderland of bright lights, sparkling ornaments, sleigh bells, toys, winter scenes, jolly Santas and more -- all accompanied by beloved holiday melodies.

programme.title: Baby Einstein Classics
programme.desc: From the day their eyes open, babies begin to observe new things around the world and about themselves. Baby Da Vinci From Head to Toe presents them with a playful and interactive introduction to the human body in three languages -- English, Spanish, and French. With a whimsical blend of adorable puppets, real children and animals and famous works of art, this captivating program also features upbeat classical music.

programme.title: Baby Einstein New Classics
programme.desc: Meet fascinating creatures from across the globe and learn to run, hop, and crawl like your favorite animal friends. Dance and sing along to discover animal names and the native habitats of the world's most unique creatures. See real-life images of lions and zebras on the African savannah, then learn animal names through repetition and narration, or simply watch your favorite animals play!

programme.title: Farmees Movie Compilation
programme.desc: Farmees brings you the best of nursery rhymes and kids songs. Your kids are sure to enjoy!

programme.title: Dinobees Funny Cartoon Show for Kids
programme.desc: Welcome to #Dinobees, a funny show for kids. It revolves around a family that discovers and adopts three dinosaurs. This comedy cartoon is perfect entertainment for toddlers.Dinobees has some amazing 3d animations. Get ready to have great jungle adventures with cartoon animals and dinosaurs.This entertaining playlist is a collection of videos for children like Fire Discovery, Fruits of Labour, Magical Berries, Catch me If You can, Glue Trap, Mis Splits, I Wanna Fly, The Gophers, Baby Sitter, Frozen Lake, Bubble Trouble, Where's My Water and many more videos.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: When Robotnik puts a meteor on a collision course with a village, Sonic is forced to compete in the Robolympics to save the village. After a series of wild, booby trapped events, Sonic is victorious.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: When outlaws chase a sheriff out of a western town, Sonic takes over. But the outlaws are robots built by Robotnik. And when they capture a pretty possum of a saloon girl, Sonic meets Robotnik for a showdown.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: First in a four-part Time Travel series. Robotnik invents a time machine to go back in time and gather four powerful gemstones. His first attempt is on the high seas, but Sonic goes into hyper-Sonic-overdrive, and reverses time to go after him. ...

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Robotnik timetravels back to Mobigypt, makes himself a pharoah, and enslaves everbody in an effort to find the gemstone of immortality.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Brandon Quark, an evil half-man, half-duck genius challenges, then teams up with Robotnik to capture Sonic. But Sonic knows their weakness, and pits the Badnik geniuses against one another, and sets himself free.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog, Adventures of
programme.desc: Sonic and Tails help out an Owl/Chilidog Diner owner, named Bert Whoo, when Robotnik uses his henchbots to try to collect protection money. When the henchbots prove to be no match for Sonic.

Rainbow Ruby uses her magic suitcase and transforms into a doctor and cares for him.

programme.desc: Ling Ling is worried about the play planned for tonight. There are no costumes ready for the villagers so it's hard to figure out which roles they're playing. programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: After nearly 500 years, French is still the official language of this North American city. How has this UNESCO World Heritage Site retained its Frenchness after all this time? Why does the Chteau Frontenac hotel look like a French castle, and what does it have to do with the Canadian railway? Whats so curious about Rue Saint-Jean, Place Royal and Petit-Champlain?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Englands Great West Way follows the ancient path from London to Bristol. We take the train & get curious at stops along the way: Stonehenge, Baths masonic architecture, the Roman Baths, the S.S. Great Britain, sample some curious pub pies, learn some cockney rhyming slang, and visit some charming villages too.

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why is La Grande-Place so gilded? How did Brussels chocolate legacy begin? Where can you walk through the pages of a comic book? How did Flanders become such a source of Master artists?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why is there a giant wall in the middle of Cardiff? Who built Cardiff Castle? And Coch Castle? Why does Cardiff have so many Victorian and Georgian arcades? What is a bara brith? And laverbread? (Seriously, what is it?). Where did Princess Diana give her first public speech in Welsh?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: How did Manchester kick-start Railway Mania in the Industrial Age? What is Manchesters Water Palace? What is so curious about the architecture of the John Rylands and Chethams Libraries? Why is a bee the symbol of Manchester? Which pub owner shooed her patrons out with a broom? Why is the National Football Museum in Manchester?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: How did Hong Kong get its name? What is so junk-y about a junk boat? Get curious about the Big Buddha, the Po Lin Monastery, and the Wisdom Path on Lantau Island. Then, the Kung Wo Tofu Factory, to learn the centuries-old tofu-making technique. We also take you inside a Tin Hau Temple, the Man Mo Temple, and a traditional Qipao dress-making shop.

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: We get curious about the stunning natural landscape of Wales. We visit Brecon Beacons National Park, Pembrokeshire National Park, St. Davids (the smallest city in Britain), Hay-onWye (WalesTown of Books), the Victorian seaside resort town of Tenby, and lots and lots of curious castles.

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why did the English choose Jamestown, Yorktown and Williamsburg, Virginia as their early American settlements? Why did King William III and Queen Mary II establish a university here? What was life like for the colonists here? What crazy concoctions are to be found in a colonial apothecary?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: After nearly 500 years, French is still the official language of this North American city. How has this UNESCO World Heritage Site retained its Frenchness after all this time? Why does the Chteau Frontenac hotel look like a French castle, and what does it have to do with the Canadian railway? Whats so curious about Rue Saint-Jean, Place Royal and Petit-Champlain?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Englands Great West Way follows the ancient path from London to Bristol. We take the train & get curious at stops along the way: Stonehenge, Baths masonic architecture, the Roman Baths, the S.S. Great Britain, sample some curious pub pies, learn some cockney rhyming slang, and visit some charming villages too.

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why is La Grande-Place so gilded? How did Brussels chocolate legacy begin? Where can you walk through the pages of a comic book? How did Flanders become such a source of Master artists?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: Why is there a giant wall in the middle of Cardiff? Who built Cardiff Castle? And Coch Castle? Why does Cardiff have so many Victorian and Georgian arcades? What is a bara brith? And laverbread? (Seriously, what is it?). Where did Princess Diana give her first public speech in Welsh?

programme.title: Curious Traveler
programme.desc: How did Manchester kick-start Railway Mania in the Industrial Age? What is Manchesters Water Palace? What is so curious about the architecture of the John Rylands and Chethams Libraries? Why is a bee the symbol of Manchester? Which pub owner shooed her patrons out with a broom? Why is the National Football Museum in Manchester?

programme.title: Classic Fireplace
programme.desc: The beloved traditional yule log that started it all. Gentle flames dance over seasoned wood, filling your home with nostalgic warmth and timeless holiday comfort.

programme.title: Campfire Fireplace
programme.desc: Experience the timeless charm of a log pile ablaze with cozy firelight. Natural wood textures and subtle pops bring a cabin-in-the-woods feel to your living room.
